data.text: I found one leftover brace from the splice boundary. I’m removing that stray terminator now so the new method and the existing helper declarations line up cleanly.

I caught one scope issue before compiling: the preflight `setExit` closure still sits above the `state` declaration, so it can’t touch the state directly. I’m reverting that line and keeping the loop-end reason on the local variable that `Run` already owns; the iteration method will still sync its own exit reason into that local after each pass.
I’ve got the structural splice done. Next I’m formatting the file to force a parse check, then I’ll run the agent package tests to see whether anything else in the extraction needs a correction pass.

data.text: I found the stop-reason regression: the iteration defer was copying a stale local back over the state after `applyStop` had already set it. I’m removing that overwrite so the state-owned exit reason survives to `loop.end` and the result object.

I’ve fixed the stop-reason overwrite. The package tests are running again; if they clear, I’ll move straight to the staged pre-commit gate, and if they still fail, I’ll use the failing assertions to tighten the refactor further.
